review_text:
Wir waren zu zweit über das Wochenende in einer Queen Site Hütte. Es war gut ausgestattet und es fehlte nichts. Allerdings war das Geschirr nicht richtig sauber und es es gab Ecken wo man besser sauber machen sollte. Das Schlafzimmer und Bad waren aber sehr sauber.
Außerdem ging unsere Klimaanlage nicht. Es wurde überhaupt nicht warm. Wir kamen erst abends an und hatten somit einen Self- Check in was sehr einfach und problemslos verlief. Allerdings mussten wir aufgrund des Problems mit der Klimaanlage in der Kälte sitzen.
Wir waren aber umso glücklicher, dass das Bett eine Heizmatratze hatte. Dies hat unsere Nacht gerettet.
Am nächsten Tag konnten wir der Rezeption Bescheid geben und sie gaben uns kleinen Heizlüfter. Sie waren sehr höflich und hilfsbereit.
Die Lage ist top. Die Promenade mit Shops und Restaurants sind zu Fuß erreichbar und Attraktionen in der Natur sind mit dem Auto auch schnell erreichbar.

Very disappointed with our recent stay at Amaroo Caravan Park over New Year’s (31 Dec 2025 – 3 Jan 2026).

We have stayed here multiple times over the years and unfortunately since the change in management the overall standard and upkeep of the park has declined significantly.

The amenities block was consistently dirty throughout our stay, with hair and other rubbish left in the showers for days, bugs and cobwebs around the basins and mirrors, and leaking shower heads spraying water everywhere. The general upkeep of the park was also poor, with overgrown gardens, weeds and cobwebs visible throughout the park and around cabins.

There also appeared to be little to no management presence after hours, with multiple groups making excessive noise well past 11pm on several nights without any intervention.

What was most disappointing was the complete lack of response after our stay. I attended the office before departure to raise concerns however nobody was there. I then sent a formal email outlining our experience and requesting contact, followed by a phone call, and have still received no response.

Considering the premium price charged during peak season, the standard provided was well below expectations. We visited friends staying at another caravan park on the island who paid less for a longer stay and their park was maintained to a far higher standard.

Unfortunately, Amaroo no longer feels like the family-friendly park we remembered and, based on this experience, I would not recommend staying here in its current condition.

Due to the photos, the reviews and the location we booked a stay for 5 nights at Amaroo Park in Cowes.  The young man at the desk (Taylor?) was friendly and helpful. Aside from the very front of the premises where the gardens and smaller apartments look neat, the rest of the property is very run down. The apartment that we booked, a 'Premier apartment' was very small - half the size of a normal unit, but well appointed and self contained. It was suitable really only for a single person - or a couple for 1 night. We stayed for 5 nights and the accommodation was claustrophobic and overpriced we believe - at almost $700. We only spent some evenings in the apartment, as we were busy during our stay. The rooms were clean - but no doona cover. And the very bare essentials for cutlery and crockery, for 2 people. 2 towels were provided and no face washers - we had to keep asking for clean towels. The garden surrounds were uninviting and very poorly kept. Many of the unit rooves had grass growing in the gutters due to tree leaf build-up. The property overall looks very tired and the maintenance is poor. Compared to other accommodation places we saw in Cowes this place could best be described as 'Bogan-ville' and the only reason we would rate this 3 out of 5 stars and not 2, is due to the staff. The restaurant (Mon Dieu) adjoining the property was most enjoyable also. Compared to other places we have stayed at over the years, for price and value, this one is a NO go.

Late booking for 2 nights, chose this park due to its central location and cheaper rate. I was aware of its change of owner and the decline in its upkeep. Sadder still was the vibe of the place now and the lack of welcoming or warmth from the new staff. The man in reception’s customer service skills didn’t extent beyond asking for cc and ID cards and handing over the park map. No pleasantries or interest in their visitors beyond taking their money was the impression I was left with.
The was also no consideration or respect from the cleaning staff for visitors not checking out in the mornings. I also clean for work (in aged care) and while you can’t avoid the noise of a vacuum cleaner, you can certainly minimise noise by not dragging chairs across floors and quietly opening/shutting doors and windows and not calling out to other cleaners as they pass by.
The lack of response from management to negative feedback is a massive red flag as well. I won’t be spending my hard earned cash on a place that cares so little about the island or its guests.

after I booked a powered site through Amaroo’s website, I was a little concerned after seeing some negative reviews in the past few months. however, it turned out to be a very pleasant experience for me and my partner. the BBQ area was clean and easy to use, and the toilets and showers were okay (although the top of the hand soap was dusty and the sink had a bit of rust—housekeeping could probably do a bit better there). the hot showers worked well (the best part!) and the staff were helpful.
Very clean rooms! Comfortable and location is quite near to cafes etc
Great spacious camping sites with power, walking distance from the town center, rabbits running around. Nice pool

Much too expensive for what it is.  It is poky.  The kitchen supplies were shocking.  Garbage bin with no lid/health and safety hazard.  Cutlery all odd 2 knives, 2 steak knives, 2 forks, 2 teaspoons.  No mugs.  2 cups no saucers. 3 odd wine glasses, you couldn't both have the same size glass.  No tongs. For washing up they had cut a sponge scourer into thirds.  Clock doesn't work.  Big splash of ? down window on inside  really only half a cabin.  Very tight .

Disappointed ☹️
For $240 per night
I was not expecting:
- grass growing through the front porch
- curtains hanging off the rails (broken and missing hooks)
- net curtains that had been cut in jagged half with scissors
- pissy toilet seat (cleaned on the top of the seat but not under)
- no light in the toilet
- only one wine glass!
- a non-working clock
- dirty old couch that looked like it had come from Lifeline, and no coffee table
- a dribble of a washing up liquid in the bottom of a bottle
- having booked a 2 bed villa, to find bedroom two closed with a sticker on it saying ‘due to an upgrade’ we weren’t to use it and if we do it will cost $80 per night!!
However, bathroom, towels and bedding were clean and bed was comfortable.
Shower was hot and water pressure was good.
Suggest the owner maybe spends a couple of hundred bucks at Kmart and Bunnings and this would’ve been a very different review.
Emplacement très bien rapport qualité prix en camping car sanitaires propres à proximité du centre

The only good review about this caravan park is location.|Pool was green, toilet blocks are old, rundown and only cleaned once a day. Bins always overflowing. Grounds are unkept, lawns not mowed. I am sure owners do not walk around their park, otherwise maintenance would be done, if they had any respect. Gravel was annoying to walk on and always spread onto concrete pads.
Just, very convenient. Highly recommended for its peaceful nature,location, cleanliness, supportive nature of the stuff and price.

Wir waren mit unserem Camper für eine Nacht vor Ort. Kleine süße Anlage mit freien Wlan. Die Sanitären Anlagen sind etwas in die Jahre gekommen, aber sauber. 2 Waschmaschine u. 2 Trockner sind vorhanden.
Der Strand und eine kleine Einkaufsstrasse sind ca.850 m entfernt. Ebenfalls in der Nähe sind 2 Supermärkte. Aldi und Coles.

Die Hilfsbereitschaft der Mitarbeiter ist exzellente. Taylor hat uns großartig geholfen, als unsere Camper Probleme mit der Seitentür gemacht hat. Dafür alleine würde ich schon mehr als 5 Sterne geben.

Vielen Dank für alles
The best way to enjoy our stay.
Clean,comfortable, quiet. They have blackout curtains. Coin operated washers  and dryers. Outside line. Friendly staff.

This place used to be my favourite caravan park, great location and not too big. We have bought a caravan since our last stay, just went to book and $220 per night for a powered site is absolutely ludicrous. No wonder this is one of the only places with sites still available for the labour day weekend. I get rising the prices for public holidays but this is just greedy in my opinion.
Stay was fantastic.wanted to stay there for more days.
Spacious clean comfortable..

I stayed in a king cabin. It was exactly as shown in the pictures on the website. Despite a few cosmetic patch jobs, being an older cabin, and a lack of a full length mirror I had a very pleasant stay. The cabin was clean and tidy. The king bed was extremely comfortable with crisp clean sheets. Good pressure in the shower but note it is a very small moulded type shower in a small bathroom but fine for me. The park was well maintained. The pool was clean and lovely. The staff were friendly and welcoming. The location is perfect. Walking distance to beach and town and Coles supermarket just up the road. This is an older park and there’s a few little things they need to update but nothing I found excessive or that made my stay negative. I would not hesitate at all to stay here again.

This place is brilliant.  Great location and friendly staff.  The cabin was very clean and the bed was very comfortable.  We stayed in a pet friendly cabin and our puppy loved it.  Will definitely be back.

Very disappointing room, no cooking facility  (no toaster or microwave) there was a kettle. We had two bread plates, two knives, two forks and one soup spoon, one teaspoon, and bench that was suitable to stand at, no stools, and two chairs. 75% of the room was the queen size bed and 10% was the bench area. There was a very narrow hall which was the toilet, wash basin, and shower closet. The pricing was ridiculous, and yes we know it was a public holiday weekend, but we could have stayed two nights at crown for the same money in a 4+ star hotel. Do your homework before thinking about this place.

What a rip off? I understand it was Australia Day weekend but for $330/night we expected more. The main room was very small, the toilet, shower and basin in a tiny really cramped space. The bench top was okay but with the chairs supplied, it was unworkable. It served for nothing but to put things on. Chairs supplied way too low, stools would have at least given a seating area suitable to eat at. 4 sugar & coffee satches for the long weekend, no salt or pepper and one table spoon, one knife and fork still had food on it. Very basic for the money paid. The fridge wasn’t cleaned, and there was food left in it. Really disappointing!!

I don’t really understand what we’re paying for here.

We stayed with a campervan, and not all powered sites have access to water, which wasn’t clear at the time of booking. The women’s shower block is very old and poorly maintained, with random holes throughout, and it was dirty during our stay.

None of the bathrooms had soap available — instead there was only disinfectant placed near the sinks, which isn’t an adequate substitute.

There was fresh graffiti in the men’s bathroom block (I’ll include a photo), which, combined with the lack of upkeep, raised some concerns about safety and supervision around the park.

The hot water stopped working in the women’s bathroom block around 5pm, just as reception closed. To be fair, after reporting the issue it was resolved in about 30 minutes by changing the gas, but it still wasn’t ideal timing.

The boom gate was never closed or operating at any point in the evening, which added to our safety concerns. Some guests’ dogs were also free to roam around the park — the dog we encountered seemed friendly, but it still didn’t feel appropriate.

Overall, the facilities and management don’t match what you’d expect for a paid stay.
Grubby and old. They didnt tell me I would be in a really old cabin. So unfair to do this to people.  I asked for a refund for the 2nd night so I could go somewhere else but they refused.

Booked a 2 bedroom superior cabin, weeds all around, get inside and floors have not been cleaned very well at all, toilet is not very clean, there is marks under the seat and the pivot point of the seat.
There is pop corn and a TUSSUE behind the couch pillow which my 10 year old found.
I ended up wiping all surfaces, swept the floors and cleaned the toilet and shower floor!
Looks like they turn over the accomodation pretty quick and no detail to cleaning is taken into account.

The room was very very basic, basicly a box with a bed and TV, and a unsuite. No microwave or toaster till we asked, location very central to city, maintenance of garden and lawns needs some serious attention. Would not recommend this place

We thought our one bedroom cabin was great. It was a good size, the bedroom was large with a sitting area and the couch was a fold out sofa bed in the lounge which was perfect for the kids.  It was clean and comfortable. ||The pool was nice, could do with more sun loungers and I noticed there wasn’t many chairs as other people had to bring some with them to watch their kids swim. ||We also used the BBQ one night which was also good. Nice close proximity to Aldi and the centre of Cowes. We also walked to the beach at Cowes a few times. ||I would definitely stay here again.

Honestly the one of the few good things was the location being so close to the shops and the beach, other than that we were disappointed with our rooms especially considering how much we paid for the two nights (yes it is peek time). We noticed the cleanliness of the place wasn't up to standard, with stains on the Doona (looked like blood) and also the carpet. There was rubbish under the pillows of the couch and under the bed which wasnt ours.

For four people, the amendments were short, not having much body wash etc.

In our booking, I put down that we had an under 5 but there was no place for them to sleep as they weren't allowed on the bunk bed that was open
We tried to close this and it took half the day until we were able to get help to close it.

Suggestions: a good clean throughout the room, behind doors, above and shower and under the couches and bed would be fantastic. Maybe proper curtains in the bedrooms that actually close as well. The pool also needed a good clean.I feel with a bit of love in the place, it will change reviews and make it better.

What a great location, as only a short walk to the centre of Cowes and an easy drive to all the Phillip Island activities. The amenities are old style and everything worked no problems but could do with a freshen up. The lawns needed a mow also. Good service and a great cafe next door. Great value also.

Absolutely disgusting service. Spoke to us like we were children, wanted to keep our credit card at the front desk for 'security reason' and when we declined he tried to charge an extra $200! Honestly seems scammy especially considering the change of ownership and decline in stars since the change

Edit: all kind and nice on the keyboard, completely different people in person. DONT WASTE YOUR TIME
Excellent rooms. Warmers for beds available.  Within greenery homes, reasonable price. Very quite place.

Despite all the recent negative stars, I still went ahead and book the place. Good price per night, very central location and walking distance to central Cowes. Booked a King Cabin and had a good experience, clean (may need more vacuuming in small corner areas) however it's not 5 stars Hilton or Hyatt.
Overall a good 2 nights escape in central Cowes.

Feedback: more vacuuming and mopping of floors required in kitchen and bedroom. You will attract more business in the future.

I had a terrible experience at this place. When we arrived, the room was dirty and definitely not what we expected. The receptionist was extremely rude from the moment we checked in. Even though we had already booked and paid, he tried to charge us extra for a different room.
While we were trying to sort things out, he actually came into the room, told us to get out, and kicked us out on the spot. It was shocking and completely unprofessional. We asked for a refund because the way we were treated was unacceptable.
I would not recommend staying here to anyone — save yourself the stress.
Fantastic location. The park offers excellent value for money, with clean and well-maintained facilities.  Highly recommended!

Location to discover the island is good.
It’s nice to have a coffee right next to the campsite.
Otherwise it was just okay to stay there for two nights and not more.
Facilities could be cleaner and they are a bit old.
Not to good a situation rose where the manager decided to speak to the person at 11.30 pm with a very aggressive tone when it could have be addressed earlier in the day would not say that again
Very obliging staff and great room quality for money
A very good place to stay. Walking distance to shops, beach, restaurants. Definitely recommend
Just fantastic place ... rooms are clean .The area is well kept  and managed by beautiful husband and wife team .. we stay about 7  times a year .. highly recommended

Do not book this park!

This accommodation was advertised as having a dishwasher, however there was none. On arrival, the first thing I noticed was cigarette butts and an almost full ashtray left at the front of the cabin, which was already disappointing.

We advised management straight away about the missing dishwasher. The next morning, while cleaning and opening the curtains, I discovered black mould covering the window seals and sheer curtains throughout the cabin. We contacted them immediately and made the decision to check out early due to the unhygienic conditions.

The grounds of the park were also overgrown and poorly maintained, giving the overall environment a neglected feel. Check-in was cold and unwelcoming — we were barely spoken to, which is not the warm, friendly experience you expect from a caravan park. It felt nothing like any other park we’ve ever stayed at.

We requested a refund as the place was clearly not clean or safe to stay in, however they are chosing to not respond to myself and booking.com after many atempts to speak and resolve this which felt extremely offensive given the condition of the accommodation. The cabin was filthy and the presence of black mould is a serious health concern. Overall, a very disappointing and unacceptable experience.

Do not waste your money, there are much better caravan parks on the island.
Staff were hard to communicate with there was a language barrier |Cabin in poor condition and not clean |Crockery glassware minimal and not clean

Room were family friendly to live-in though bathmate should have been there to avoid water spilling around in bathroon. Quite disappointed with swimming pool since my children refuse to even enter inside since there were algaes all around. Check-in time is 2:00 pm which is also cafe closeing time. We had chance to go cafe next day but keep in mind you have to exit the park as the cafe entrance is on the douglas road. Should have been access to enter the cafe for park visitors at least. A short 10-12 mins walk to Cowes beach is a plus.

What a total rip off.not worth the $1600 for three days. I wouldn't go here again I'd rather sleep in my car or under a bridge.. I don't recommend this place at all. There are better places that is more than half price of this place. Carnt say much good things about this place.

Really needs a good clean. Dirty marks on walls. Holes in fly screens, dirty freezer, food on wall, stains on couch, not clean under the couch the cushions. Only thing I thought was very good was the bed very comfortable
Awesome

Toller Ort um auf Phillip Island zu übernachten.
Wir waren 1 Nacht dort. Da es eher etwas kälter war, wurde es auch nicht wirklich warm in der Unterkunft.
Küche ist gut und ausgerüstet um etwas zu kochen fürs Frühstück, Utensilien könnten etwas sauberer sein.

Alles in allem ein Toller Ort zu übernachten wenn man zur PinguinParade möchte.

I was looking for something affordable and was pleasantly surprised by this motel. Advertised on Wotif as the least expensive room and when I called reception, I received an even better price. Employee was friendly and efficient, room was immaculate/neat/nice smelling and had everything you could need. Communication and organisation perfect re a late check in. Surprised by other negative reviews. It’s perfectly acceptable place to stay.

This was a very disappointing stay. It is clearly under new management as what used to be a reliably spotless place to stay is now smelly and dirty.
We stayed in a king and while it very spacious, the smell of cooking (animal fats no less!) permeated everything and bordered on nauseating. Reception gave us deodorising spray which could not mask the awful odour.
The room was dirty - grime on blinds, the extraction fan was a horror show. Dirty oven front and dirty fridge. Hairy shower (yikes!).
We were very disappointed and can no longer recommend a stay here.

We stayed one night only (thankfully) on a powered site. The grass was long and full of bindies. The advertised “spotless bathrooms and renovated amenities” were filthy and not renovated since the 1970’s. I wouldn’t let my children have showers in there. Holes in walls, toilet and shower doors that wouldn’t lock. Unsecure park over night with boom gate left open all night. 2 washing machines and 2 dryers for the whole holiday park, yet the cleaning staff were using the guests only washer and dryers for their own cleaning supplies and linen. We had to wait to be able to do our own washing. Location wise, noisy with dog next door barking all night and people yelling in the streets. Zero customer service around at all due to self check in. $57 for a night, absolutely not worth it.

Booked a queen cabin.
Good bed and clean linen and towels. Cleanliness not up to par.
No clothes hangers, just 8 hooks.
Pretty pointless having the tv as you have to sign in to all the apps.
Absolutely NO SOUNDPROOFING! Could hear everything neighbours did - including pissing.
Shower only suits those under 5'9" - brained myself on door lintel and shower head.
Had to keep blinds shut at all times for privacy.
Definitely wouldn't stay again

Edit: Tom, thanks for your reply. Unfortunately I cannot reply to it directly so am using the only function available to me. I take issue with the price you have stated for the queen studio cabin. My friends booked the cabin for me (they took a king cabin which was filthy and stank) and they paid $152 per night on my behalf, so either you're being disingenuous or you have vastly overcharged us and we are due a refund. Pick your poison...

And regarding the noise from my neighbours - they were just going about their business, not being lairy or unreasonably loud so a complaint to them was totally unwarranted. The issue (which you seem to be missing) is that I could still hear everything. Net curtains would have afforded more sound deadening.

I am merely offering an honest review to enable others to make their own mind up as to whether they are willing to put up with the situation.

I appreciate this isn't what you want to hear but I do suggest that you listen to my feedback and try not to get quite so huffy.
The warmer is not warm enough and the kettle is dirty. Affordable price, ok to stay for 1 night

A group of us stayed 4 nights over the MotoGP. The first night there was a cardboard label on the shower floor of main amenities. Each night  used the same shower and on night two- 2 used bandaids and the cardboard were on the floor. The amenities block was not cleaned the entire 4 nights. A $170 a night for a powered site that is despicable. I spoke to the office and got a roundabout response that cleaner cleans at 8am every morning (sign says 11.30) and then they do a general clean and don’t clean showers or toilets- he then back pedalled and said they do, but my photos over the days prove otherwise. We were told site next to us wasn’t cleaned up as a long term stayer had been there, but the rubbish and dead grass was also on half of our site- it was just dirt, rubbish, old blankets, chewed up dog toys, even a shopping trolley. I rolled my ankle the first night as the dog had dug holes.Never ever again!

Arrived at property and told where apartment was not friendly.|Opened door dirty undies under bed couch had sand dirt, etc. floors filthy went back to reception,asked for a vacuum cleaner.|I WAS GIVEN vacuum cleaner to go and clean opening cupboards there were left over food crumbs filthy.||I went to open kitchen windows, and it was broken. I asked someone to fix it.||I was asked to bring back vacuum cleaner| to go away for 1 hour and then come back. I refused and was offered all my money back.|This was in the superior cabin
The location was good it is close to all shops and restaurants and the room is clean but just the bedrooms are a bit small
Great location holiday park, stayed in luxury family villa, but wouldn’t state it as “luxury” for the price you pay. Could have been cleaner… considering it a “new” building

Stayed here 3 times before very clean think new owners now ...still very good..resturant next door excellent food n service 6.30am/2.00pm so close to supermarkets n main street .cabins perhaps getting a bit tired but spotless .....

We went to the Cafe for brunch
It was very good and the service was good
However the supervisor was a bit gruff and off putting
Excellent location to all amenities. Happy to have chosen this accommodation.
觀賞完企鵝歸巢後駕車10分鐘左右就到了，附件有兩大超市，可以買食材煮飯。房間還很貼心地準備了電暖毯，晚上很暖和。

I have stayed here multiple times over the last 5 years, but sadly with a change of owners the condition of this property has gone downhill very quickly.

The first cabin they put me in was filthy and the smell was unbearable. Clearly they are aware of the smell as every window was wide open to air it out.

Complained to the front desk and was moved to a different cabin. Just as filthy but the smell wasn't quite as bad. Ended up going to Coles and buying a candle just to try to make being in there somewhat bearable. Of course the lighter for the stove wasn't working, so a second trip to Coles to buy matches was required. Thankfully Coles is only a block away.

It's a real shame as the old owners put in their heart and soul into maintaining this well located accommodation. Rooms were always pristinely cleaned and well appointed. Now this place would be the absolute last option on my list of accommodation near Cowes. Would rather sleep in my car...

This accommodation is well located, only minutes walk from Coles.
We took our dog and had a pet friendly cabin, which
I can't complain about except for the standard things that bother me about this type of accommodation.

Why do I feel that when you go to these places, there's a real lack of some of the most basic kitchen equipment. We didn't have any dessert spoons and only 2 cereal bowls. Now, when you book a cabin for 3, the minimum you expect is 3 of everything. There was no cheese grater, one disgusting plastic bowl, and barely anything else.
I believe there should be some regulation around what is provided in the cabins by the owners of this type of business. People do not want to travel with their kitchen sink when they go   on holiday!
Dreadful. Would not recommend.
Very clean and nice comfortable cabin , planets of room for a family of four
Good location, nice rooms and great price.
Cabin very clean. Close to shops. Very happy with stay would stay again.
Clean n convenient as supermarket is next door 5min walk. Or drive over 1min if buy a lot.. parking is free
Excellent lovely people run sooo, they are fantastic.
We had such a wonderful stay here. Lovely accommodation and pool. Right next to the beach and walk to cafes. We hope to come back again soon!

The worst Airbnb/hotel experience I’ve ever had.
I paid over $410 plus a $55 surcharge for bringing my dog, and we stayed in what was advertised as a “luxury villa” — but it was far from even comfortable.

There was a large kitchen window with no curtains, meaning no privacy at night. The place felt very stingy: we were given a single 150ml bottle of milk for three people, and the shampoo and body wash provided were barely enough for one person. Cooking ware was extremely limited, and there were no basic condiments at all.

Definitely not worth the price or the description.

The hospitality and accomodation here is awful.
We booked for a luxury villa for the capacity of 4 person. However there was only one Devondale milk provided.
All the dishes and plates weren't cleaned properly, and we had to chuck EVERYTHING in dishwasher again for a proper clean. NO condiments were provided at all.

While the location is great and the campgrounds themselves are lovely, sadly this park has gone downhill. We stayed here 2 years ago during winter 2023 and it was great. Vast comparison to this past week. In the week we stayed, the amenities were never cleaned, the floors never swept or washed, the basins never cleaned or the benches wiped down. The bin was never emptied. Twice I made comment to staff about this - the second time the guy told me to go and speak to the cleaners myself! That's not my job, it's his. And then on the very morning we are leaving, they decide to clean the toilets just before check out time, so I couldn't access the toilets at all. There are cleaning hours on the doors but definitely not adhered to. Also the amenity room doors were left wedged open, so that leaves and grit blew in, and anyone could have been in the toilets at night, because the doors weren't kept locked. A sad affair - I hope they get the message and start looking after this place properly again. I should say, different owners to who was there 2 years ago.
Close to the main shops and eateries in Cowes. Room clean and tidy

I recently stayed at Amaroo Park in Cowes, Phillip Island, and had a fantastic experience! The park offers a variety of accommodation options, including villas, studios, and powered sites, catering to different budgets and preferences. I opted for a villa, and it was clean, comfortable, and well-equipped with everything I needed for a relaxing stay.

The park is in a perfect location, just a short distance from the beach and the main street of Cowes, where you can explore local cafes, restaurants, and shops. It’s also only a 10-minute drive to popular attractions like the Penguin Parade and the Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit, making it a great base for exploring the island.

One of the highlights of my stay was the friendly and welcoming staff. They were always available to offer recommendations or assist with anything I needed. The park itself is well-maintained, with a solar-heated pool, BBQ area, and a games room that added extra fun to my stay.

The peaceful atmosphere and well-kept grounds made it a perfect spot to relax after a day of sightseeing. I highly recommend Amaroo Park for anyone visiting Phillip Island, whether for a short getaway or a longer stay. It offers excellent value for money, with great facilities and a location that can’t be beaten.
Service is excellent

Absolutely horrid! Please don’t stay here.
Room smelt like urine. We turned on the heater which made the room smell worse.
Bed was sheet less and had crumbs in the bed.
Pull out was disgusting, clearly not cleaned in years.
Left the same night.
There are MUCH better options.
Save yourself the money don’t stay here!

Good value for money, clean and awesome hot shower. However the pictures online is much better than in real life. It’s basic and clean, and you get what you pay for. The sofa looked a tad old and used. Other than that, there’s hairdryer and basic toiletries. Free parking.
友善又熱情的櫃檯人員
Great location but cabins could do with a refurbishment and good clean

Le camping est sympa, plutôt bien organisé avec tout ce qu’il faut. Cependant, c’est extrêmement bruyant. Et petit plus pour les sanitaires qui sont encore moins propres que des toilettes d’air d’autoroute… entre les traces excréments, le sang sur les poubelles, les poubelles qui débordent et les touffes de poils et cheveux dans la douche, on ose meme pas poser ses affaires pour ce laver…
Tolle "Villa" zum günstigen Preis.  Sehr zu empfehlen

IMPORTANT PLEASE READ FOR YOUR OWN SAKE – Long Review|I could NOT agree more with Daniel K’s bad review and being semi permanent caravanners travelling all up the East coast to Bundaberg and along the Murray River and South Australia, THIS IS THE WORST RUN PARK  we have ever stayed at.  At the outset, apparently this place was owned by “an English family” but it certainly is not now and run by Chinese people .  This is important because from the reviews that are positive, there is no doubt they are not for the current owners.  We moved from the NRMA park 800 meters away as we could not extend our stay because they were fully booked.  Walked in to the reception at Amaroo, waited, rang the bell over 15 minutes and no one appeared.  Called the number and got an asian female and it was hard to understand her so I concluded the phone call stating very clearly, “we are in your reception and we want to book in”.  We could not understand exactly what she said so I repeated “we are in reception…can you come and help us please”.  She said “yes, yes” to which I repeated myself again to make sure she understood and got a “yes yes” again.  After 20 minutes (no exaggeration), still no one came so we walked out in disgust..  We drove in to the park and whilst every other park we tried were full, this park had 2 caravans in it (red flag).  Anyway, we had no choice so I booked for 4 days, spoke to an Asian male and asked if we could check in at 10am as we needed to vacate the NRMA by 10am.  He was much easier to understand and said “no problem, you can check in anytime”.  We arrived and my wife went in to check in and was told that we can not check in as it is too early.  She came out and I went in and after pointing out our phone conversation the previous day including that the park was empty…he agreed.  First, there is plenty of grass but the larger sites which we needed leave you stepping out on to dirt so you need a floor mat.  No one stays there so why haven’t they fixed this (minor issue). Second, the dump point is 90mm pipe and that’s it.  No proper dump point and only meters from a cabin.  The amenities need serious attention but worse, they do not clean them.  For 3 consecutive days, the same toilet seat had stains.  I could go on about amenities but conclude with, they are a disgrace.  We observed the cleaner attend to the cabin near us for 4 days after each new person moved in and having owned a motel for 4 years, he was far too quick to have cleaned the rooms properly and on our last morning, I asked the young family what the cabin was like and he said “ok but it was not clean”.  This place is a disgrace and I would never stay there ever again and it is no surprise that even over the weekend, there was hardly anyone there and the couple beside us agrees with our opinion and said he also only booked because everywhere else was full.  Don’t say you have not been warned.

Lovely .I went to visit my friend who was staying there for the day and the cabin she was staying in with her son .the cabin was clean and spacious and my friend pushed me in the wheelchair to the reception to say I was staying for the night and the guy said don't worry about paying extra it's ok. 🥰The bed was comfortable and I had the longest and best sleep in years 9.5 hrs !🤩

Fantastic being able to stay in such a great location, everything is walking distance. The staff member at the office was lovely to deal with and the rooms are very clean, and as a bonus actually have a big enough couch for everyone staying.

We stayed for one night in the luxurious cabin. The cabin is new, clean and great. The beds were comfy and we also appreciated the large kitchen. But the inventory was incomplete. There were plates and cutlery missing for a 4th person, which was annoying. I found out after office hours, so wasn't able to get them. Also, this cabin (NR 15) is right next to a private house. And they own a dog, which decided to bark in the middle of the night. Not Amaroo's fault, but it woke us up in the middle of the night. And all windows have blinders, except the kitchen. So, there goes some of your privacy.

Great location with Coles within walking distance, by the way.

We got a powered site. There was a couple ditches in the ground that me and my wife had fallen into a few times that’s hard to see from the overgrown grass covering it. When I told the receptionist in the morning she had a look but when we came back that night, nothing had been done. My wife tripped again following morning. Luckily we didn’t twist our ankles.
We had also pointed out rubbish that had been lying around that was there before we came. Also nothing was done about it.
Seems they don’t care about the safety of their customers nor the tidiness of their park.

Slightly older, but the price, location, cleanliness and service has been spot on.

Thanks for having us.
Very poor management. Dump point out of order for whole week. Facilities unkept. Grounds very shabby. Not recommended.
We stayed there for a night last week and it was really comfortable stay.Rooms were clean,lounge and kitchen were tidied up very well.We loved our stay.

We just stayed two nights and are extending it to an extra night. The guy who booked us in was very friendly and helpful. Got a great deal on one of their modern cabins. Would thoroughly recommend a stay here if you are visiting Philip Island. Tucked away nicely from main street but only a short walk to all shops , beach and amenities.

The absolute worst caravan park in Australia, run by incompetent staff, in particular "Tom" or "Tang". Called and booked in two nights accomodation on Sunday January 26th for the following weekend, January 31st and February 2nd. Gave credit card details over the phone, received confirmation via email. Drove 2.5 hours from Melbourne only to be told there is no room! Apparently it was a "pre-invoice" (not sure what that is) and that we didn't confirm, despite email saying: Dear Daniel Thank you for your booking. Please see attached invoice for your reference. We look forward to see you soon!Kind regards,Amaroo Holiday Park. I replied to the email to confirm the price including pet cleaning fee, but received no response.

Absolute disgrace. Avoid this park at all costs

Closest caravan park to the main street. Walking distance to supermarkets, restaurants and pier.
Good size powered sites. Pool has lots of shade so perfect for warm days. Cafe at the front door breakfast and lunch. Easy check in. Well priced. Can't wait to come back.

My husband and I stayed here for one night on the 23rd Jan 2025, thank goodness we didn’t book for longer.
Booking this one night was a nightmare, firstly they sent us the invoice with a totally different name and date so had to contact them to rectify this, then we had to give our credit card details 3 times as the guy couldn’t understand us.
We had to contact them more than once to get the code to enter as we were arriving after reception closed, we didn’t need to bother as upon arrival the boom gate was open and stayed open all night, we also don’t need the code for the bathroom as the doors were wedged open.
Our site stunk of urine and wasn’t exactly clean, the supposed updated bathroom facilities were horrid as per the photos attached. The pool was green and the gate was wide open and to close it you had to scrape it across the concrete, we closed it for safety reasons as their were kids running around, totally against pool fencing laws in Victoria, the next morning the gate was open again this is an accident waiting to happen.
Personally I wouldn’t recommend this park to anyone.

We were stopping  on a caravan  site.  The site was good. However the toilet and shower block were not at all clean.  Think problem is they are cleaned at 11.30am. Obviously  the block is used on the evening more and when you go to shower  and use toilet at 9am they are not at all clean.  The bins in toilet  cubicle  and outside  showers are overfilled ( unpleasant) the shower floors have clumps  of hair, sand etc. The bench seat inside shower was dirty.
We have stopped in lot caravan  parks and shower blocks are cleaned  earlier  in the mornings.  So really think the owners need to rethink  the clean times. The price of the pitch was reasonable  for being  on January, as school hols  time.
The pool although some one came in the  morning  to scoop the leaves plus off the  top the pool water was very  cloudy almost greenish. Asked at reception , told it was given a complete  clean  once a week. Surely  that should be more  during  the busy season.

Oh wow. Picked this up for a bargain just before Christmas thinking better then nothing. The people running this place a so wonderful polite and accommodating. the facilities are clean and well maintained and the cabins are just prefect. Right behind Coles and the main shopping strip smack bang in the middle of everything but quite. For families this place is fantastic. Wont disappoint. Thank you to the wonderful staff

Totally loved this hotel and was super glad to have chose it for my family of 4. It has 2 bedrooms, one kitchenette and a nice living room. The whole place is clean and cozy. Kitchen could have been better equipped with some bowls though. Else everything is really good. It's walking distance to Coles, Cowes Jetty and the night streets lined with bars and restaurants. Check in and check out was easy. Free parking just outside our villa and we saw wild rabbits in the premise when we return from our Penguin Parade tour. I really like this hotel very much. Wifi was average but don't expect high speed in this area.
The room is clean and has all basic amenities. Free parking area directly outside the room. Convenient location

Nice clean rooms and bedding. Had a nice set up. good night stay. Has smart TV. Kettle with tea/coffee and a container of salt/pepper and picnic ware which would be very handy. Staff was lovely and called to notify me about after hour check in- Thank you Tom.

Great location and a small quiet park with decent size sites.

Negatives is the amenities are not clean and extremely out dated. The pool isn't heated either.
Made a booking through booking.com. The park requested us to cancel one day before our stay.

We recently stayed here, and it was nothing short of amazing! The gardens and lawns are immaculately maintained—walking around felt like a serene escape. The staff went above and beyond to make our stay perfect, accommodating our every need with such warmth and professionalism.

Also a special thank you to Tom for sorting out the extra night stay for us, arranging an extra key for us and so much more.

The cabins were spotless, beautifully designed, and equipped with everything we could possibly need for a comfortable stay. It was evident that a lot of care and attention goes into maintaining every aspect of this property.

This place truly exceeded our expectations, and we can’t wait to return. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful, top-notch getaway!
Filthy.

Close to town and very tranquil.  Friendly staff who gave us a good restaurant recommendation too. Room was practical and well equipped.  Tiny bathroom but sufficient.  Ceiling Fan was pleasant and so was the modern TV and Heat pump.  Comfy beds and bedding too. Reasonably priced.  Good parking in front of cabin. Love the trees.

Front desk were very helpful, and happy to sort out the room, before I got there..very roomy, and comfortable beds, great kitchen facilities too, with tea, coffee, milk supplied..will definitely be back on my next trip to Phillip Island 😊
電子レンジや調理器具あり。まな板無し。

The cabins are spotlessly clean. Bed linen is fresh & crisp. The "entertainment" room adjoining the office would be perfect for a growing family. Undercover outdoor kitchen is available. Easy walking distance to many cafes & restaurants.
Great place and staff were very friendly and helpful 👍

There was a private parking spot in front of the house, Room was comfort,clean and got everything you'll need for comfortable staying such as cooking appliance,towel,soap,shampoo,etc. But unfortunately there was no dishwashing liquid provided.
Ho pernottato una notte in questa struttura a.due passi dal centro...camera molto pulita  personale accogliente ...da consigliare sicuramente...

Room was warm,quiet and very clean
Highly recomend staying here

We stayed in a Queen studio with our 16 month old- there was ample space for her porta cot at the end of our end and space for us to have down time without feeling too crowded. We only stayed for one night and didn't spend too much time at the site but when we were there it was comfortable and quiet, very modern facilities.

We had such a wonderful stay. Lisa was so helpful with sorting out our last minute changes to our accommodation. Such a great place for families and pets. Our children had the best time using the campsite facilities. Loved the kind staff and a very clean and tidy environment. Would definitely recommend.

OMG THIS PLACE IS WONDERFUL!
Please don't change a thing.
This is a beautiful park, sites are a very good size, amenities are fabulously clean. You are literally a short walk from EVERYTHING food and shopping wise.
Even the bunnies 🐰  were friendly.
Thank you for a wonderful stay ❤️
Will recommend to family, friends and everyone in between.
See wikki camps. The reviews are correct

Stayed on the campground, and this is on of the top campgrounds we have stayed in 🇦🇺 so far, it is a small and quiet campground with friendly managers and no problem arriving late, the toilets, and showers are very clean. Walking distance to Coles and a nice cafe. We highly recommend staying here when visiting Philip Island.
Great place to stay
Beautiful cabins with a porch to enjoy your morning coffee. 10min walk to the beach. 5min walk to Coles. Very friendly and helpful staff. Very nice pool with comfy seating.

Such a beautiful place to stay at Cowes. We stayed in a 2 bedroom villa, which was so clean and had all the amenities required for a short stay. The place is really well maintained  and is very close to supermarkets and restaurants. Our girls loved watching the birds from the deck and also the cute in-house bunnies that hop by often. This was definitely a good find for us and we are sure to be back 😊
We love Amaroo, it is our favourite place to come as a small family. Perfect location and nice rooms

Love the quiet atmosphere and I enjoyed the stay so much. The cabin is very cozy and really clean. The hosts’ service is great and responsive. For the location, it is very convenient and near to the main shopping road.
Cancelled our stay with zero communication about it.
Great place to stay..very clean maintained park..Cabin was perfect and very clean, fans in both bedrooms..aircon as well in living area..
Back there again next year.

Really lovely small caravan/cabin park.  Cleanest cabins we've ever stayed in.  The hosts were just lovely and had plenty of time for friendly chats.  Loved the small quiet atmosphere of the park.  It was a quiet and laid back place to stay and was only about 50m from the main shopping strip.  Only thing we could think of that would improve this place would be if there was a play park for the little kids.  There was a swimming pool which was really nice for the kids though.  It was also really convenient to have the Mon Dieu Cafe right there attached to the cabin park too.
The cabins were beautifully designed, clean, and neat. The Amaroo Cafe breakfast was exceptional

I only stayed here for the 1 night but was perfect! Firstly it’s in a perfect location, only about 10 mins to the penguin parade. The staff are very friendly and welcoming to all guests. The studio cabin itself is spacious, clean, modern and fully stocked with the necessary equipment for a great stay. Free wifi worked a treat as well. Highly recommend for anyone planning to stay on Phillip island for any amount of time! P.s thank you for the amazing breakfast recommendation :)
Very clean, comfortable 2 bedroom cabin. Kitchen was fully equipped, bathroom spacious. Loved the deck. Have already booked a return stay. Will definitely be recommending to family and friends.
All good.
Small to medium park. Very well staffed and clean.

We had another fabulous stay here. The staff are so friendly and helpful. Cannot fault the cabins and the cleanliness throughout. Love being so close to town, we could walk everywhere. We will keep coming back again and again. Thanks Amaroo Park for a wonderful stay 😊
Best park ever. Managers were exceptional - always going above and beyond to accomodate and help out. Highly recommend staying at the this beautiful park.

Camping très agréable: bel emplacement pour notre tente,  sanitaires et cuisine ouverte bien équipés et très propre. Les gérants sont vraiment très accueillants. Le camping est très bien situé, on peut faire les courses à pied. Les enfants ont adoré la piscine et la salle de jeux. Ce camping est idéal pour les familles.
Great location and very clean facilities. Staff is so friendly and the bunny  hopping around is the cutest ever. Makes me want to adopt one

We were very impressed by this caravan park.  The location is very convenient plus the park managers friendly and helpful. The cabin looked brand new with excellent amenities. Such a peaceful setting too. Thoroughly enjoyed our short stay thankyou.
Seems to be brand new villas in this central location at Cowes
Nice little holiday park with great care takers. Friendly staff and the park is very clean. Dog friendly.
Friendly, Clean amenities, managed to squeeze us in on a long weekend, we hadn't booked in advance😬
Saubere und ruhige Anlage.
A nice place to relax.
Very convenient to town

Beautiful clean park. Caravan sites well maintained. Bbq area spotless.
Staff friendly and helpful.
Great location, within walking distance to the main street.

Great place to stay
Thanks see you next time
So close to everything. Not a resort but nice pool and proximity to shops and beach is awesome.
It's very very clean and very good service. I highly recommend to come out and stay at Amaro caravan park. I'm sure we will come again and again.

Lovely caravan park with an easy walk to town or the jetty. Immaculately clean bathrooms, camp kitchen, games room and a pool. Free wifi. Friendly hosts happy to offer assistance and advice the local highlights.

Great location, 10 min walk to the main town with restaurants and waterfront. AMAZING cafe on premises with absolutely great food (I am Melbourne foodie 😂) and great coffee. Very very quiet at night. We had regular bungalow with 2 bedrooms. Living room is quite modest, but we were there just to sleep really so it didn’t bother us.

The cabin was perfect for 4 adults. We arrived at 5pm on Sunday without Booking and the staff has been really flexible to check us in.
Our stay was perfect.

Great park when we don’t want to travel too far. Just off the Main Street, good sized powered sites.
Owner flat out keeping the place looking mint.

Great place for a short trip to Philip Island. Located walking distance to everything you need during your stay from grocery store to restaurants and cafes. Room came with fully stocked kitchen. The only downside is rather early check out time at 10 am but besides that a very nice place to stay for a reasonable price.
Friendly and clean room👍👍👍
Just behind the shops. Can walk to town with ease. Beautiful fairly new cabin. Well appointed, comfortable beds. Cant recommend highly enough. Thanks
Great location,  loved our cabin. Host where friendly.  Great location.
